text:
Admission Date:  1979-08-02              Discharge Date:   2013-03-22

Date of Birth:  2008-05-26             Sex:   M

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Shellfish

Attending:Jenna
Chief Complaint:
Chest pain and progressive dyspnea on exertion.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
2001-07-12 Aortic Valve Replacement utilizing a 21mm CE Magna
Pericardial Valve, and Four Vessel Coronary Artery Bypass
Grafting utilizing left internal mammary to left anterior
descending with vein grafts to diagonal, first obtuse marginal
and second obtuse marginal.


History of Present Illness:
74 year old gentleman with history of heart murmur and positive
exerercise test who now has chest discomfort and dyspnea on
exertion. Cardiac catheterization was significant for severe
three vessel coronary artery disease and severe aortic stenosis.
Given the severity of his disease, Mr. Garner was transferred
to the Healthsouth Cane Creek Rehabilitation Center for surgical management.

Past Medical History:
HTN
Hypercholesterolemia
Cataracts
History of Stroke
CVD


Social History:
Lives with wife. Quit smoking in 1986. Drinks 1 glass of wine
daily.

Family History:
Denies premature coronary artery disease

Brief Hospital Course:
Mr. Garner was admitted to the Healthsouth Cane Creek Rehabilitation Center on 1979-08-02 via transfer
form Platte Valley Medical Center for surgical management of his
aortic stenosis and coronary artery disease. He was worked-up in
the usual preoperative manner. A dental consult was obtained for
oral clearance for surgery. As radiographs suggested there were
three teeth with the potential to cause bacteremia, extraction
of teeth numbers 21, 27
and 28 was performed on 1982-09-25. On 2001-07-12, Mr. Garner was
taken to the operating room where he underwent coronary artery
bypass grafting to four vessels and an aortic valve replacement
using a 21mm CE Magna Pericardial Valve. Postoperatively he was
taken to the intensive care unit for monitoring. He required a
transfusion for postoperative anemia. Later that day he awoke
neurologically intact and was extubated. He later had
respiratory failure and required reintubation. On postoperative
day one, Mr. Garner was successfully extubated without
complication. He was slowly weaned from his pressors and
inotropes. On postoperative day three, he was transferred to the
step down unit for further recovery. He was gently diuresed
towards his preoperative weight. He required local wound care
for lower extremity blisters/ulcers. The physical therapy
service was consulted for assistance with his strength and
mobility. His pacing wires and drains were removed per protocol.
Mr. Garner remained hemodynamically stable in a normal sinus
rhythm throughout his postoperative course. He continued to make
steady progress and was discharged home on postoperative day
seven. He will follow-up with Dr. Steven, his cardiologist and
his primary care physician as an outpatient.

Medications on Admission:
Norvasc 5mg QD
HCTZ 50mg QD
Zocor 40mg QD
Aspirin 325mg QD

Toprol XL 25mg QD

Discharge Instructions:
31 shower, no baths or swimming
Monitor wounds for infection - redness, drainage, or increased
pain
Report any fever greater than 101
Report any weight gain of greater than 2 pounds in 24 hours or 5
pounds in a week
No creams, lotions, powders, or ointments to incisions
No driving for approximately one month
No lifting more than 10 pounds for 10 weeks
Please call with any questions or concerns

Followup Instructions:

Dr. Miller in 05-18 weeks (81-627-3028 please call for
appointment
Dr. Campos in 1 week please call for appointment
